question: What does it mean to have a 'nested if statement' in Python?

options.A: An if statement that is placed inside another if statement
options.B: An if statement that turns your code off
options.C: An if statement that checks things in a random order
options.D: An if statement written at the very end of your file

question: When does the computer check an inner (nested) if statement?

options.A: Before checking the outer if statement
options.B: Only if the outer if statement is True
options.C: Only if the outer if statement is False
options.D: It always checks it, no matter what

question: How do you tell Python that an 'if' statement is nested inside an outer 'if' statement?

options.B: By putting extra spaces (indenting) the inner if block further to the right
options.C: By typing the word 'nested' right before it
options.D: By writing both statements on the exact same line
